2605.08044.title: Fast Byte Latent Transformer
2605.08044.paper_summary: Recent byte-level language models (LMs) match the performance of token-level models without relying on subword vocabularies, yet their utility is limited by slow, byte-by-byte autoregressive generation. We address this bottleneck in the Byte Latent Transformer (BLT) through new training and generation techniques. First, we introduce BLT Diffusion (BLT-D), a new model and our fastest BLT variant, trained with an auxiliary block-wise diffusion objective alongside the standard next-byte prediction loss. This enables an inference procedure that generates multiple bytes in parallel per decoding step, substantially reducing the number of forward passes required to generate a sequence. Second, we propose two extensions inspired by speculative decoding that trade some of this speed for higher generation quality: BLT Self-speculation (BLT-S), in which BLT's local decoder continues generating past its normal patch boundaries to draft bytes, which are then verified with a single full-model forward pass; and BLT Diffusion+Verification (BLT-DV), which augments BLT-D with an autoregressive verification step after diffusion-based generation. All methods may achieve an estimated memory-bandwidth cost over 50% lower than BLT on generation tasks. Each approach offers its own unique advantages, together removing key barriers to the practical use of byte-level LMs.
2605.08044.paper_summary_zh: 最近的位元組級語言模型（LMs）在不依賴子詞詞彙的情況下，達到了與標記級模型相當的性能，但其效用受到逐位元組自回歸生成的速度限制。我們通過新的訓練和生成技術在位元組潛在Transformer（BLT）中解決了這一瓶頸。首先，我們介紹了 BLT 擴散（BLT-D），這是一種新模型，也是我們最快的 BLT 變體，使用輔助的區塊式擴散目標與標準的下一位元組預測損失一起進行訓練。這使得推理過程能夠在每個解碼步驟中並行生成多個位元組，顯著減少生成序列所需的前向傳遞次數。其次，我們提出了兩個受投機解碼啟發的擴展，這些擴展在提高生成質量的同時，交換了一些速度：BLT 自我投機（BLT-S），在這種情況下，BLT 的局部解碼器繼續生成超出其正常區塊邊界的位元組，然後通過一次完整模型的前向傳遞來驗證這些位元組；以及 BLT 擴散+驗證（BLT-DV），該方法在基於擴散的生成後增強了 BLT-D 的自回歸驗證步驟。所有方法在生成任務中都可能實現超過 50% 低於 BLT 的估計記憶體帶寬成本。每種方法都提供了其獨特的優勢，並共同消除了位元組級 LMs 實際使用中的關鍵障礙。
2605.08044.author: Julie Kallini et.al.
2605.08044.authors: Julie Kallini, Artidoro Pagnoni, Tomasz Limisiewicz, Gargi Ghosh, Luke Zettlemoyer, Christopher Potts, Xiaochuang Han, Srinivasan Iyer
